project_overview.project_name: Livestock Productivity and Resilience Support Project (LPRES)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve productivity, commercialization, and resilience of targeted livestock production systems in Nigeria.
project_overview.beneficiaries: 1.43 million direct beneficiaries (at least 30% women), including livestock producers, pastoral and sedentary farm households, smallholder producers, and value chain actors

primary_locations: Priority focus on northern states with pastoral systems
geographic_scope.coverage_type: National with state-level implementation and performance-based staging
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Yes - partial. Project explicitly targets areas affected by herder-farmer conflicts, particularly in Northeast and North-Central zones. Component 3 specifically addresses conflict mitigation in conflict-prone areas.

key_components.description: Strengthens livestock policy and regulatory framework, improves animal husbandry and advisory services, and strengthens animal health services. Includes preparation of Livestock Master Plan, development of sub-sector policies, support for private veterinary services, and establishment of One-Health platforms.

Enhances livestock value chains through market linkages, access to finance, and Livestock Service Centers. Includes producer organization, market infrastructure, credit line, risk-sharing facility, and establishment of 7 LSCs.

Addresses drivers of herder-farmer conflict through natural resource management, pasture improvement, and conflict mitigation mechanisms. Includes water point construction, rangeland management, conflict committees, and early warning systems.
Establishes National Coordination Office and State Coordination Offices for project management, M&E, and communication.
Zero-dollar allocation mechanism to finance response to natural disasters, disease outbreaks, or other eligible emergencies.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (FMARD) at federal level; State Ministries of Agriculture and Rural Development (SMARD) at state level; Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) for access to finance component
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National Steering Committee (NSC) chaired by Minister of FMARD; National Technical Committee (NTC); State Steering Committees (SSC) and State Technical Committees (STC); National Coordination Office (NCO) and State Coordination Offices (SCOs)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers; National procurement procedures; Competitive selection for goods, works, and services; Standard Procurement Documents; Prior and post-review by World Bank
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Comprehensive M&E system with baseline, mid-term, and end-of-project surveys; Annual administrative updates; Quarterly and semi-annual progress reports; Independent Verification Agent for Performance-Based Conditions; Sex-disaggregated indicators; Potential rigorous impact evaluation on women's economic empowerment

identified_risks.risk_description: Elite capture of marketing infrastructure, governance challenges in public sector, potential for conflict to spiral out of control in implementing areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong FM and procurement arrangements; Grievance Redress Mechanism; Transparent state selection criteria; Government commitment and support

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 pandemic impacts, economic slowdown, reduced government fiscal capacity, limited access to foreign exchange for inputs and capital expenditures
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project design flexibility; Contingency Emergency Response Component; Coordination with government fiscal planning

identified_risks.risk_description: Multi-sectoral nature of interventions, coordination challenges across disparate domains, implementation across multiple states with different contexts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Intersectoral steering and technical committees; Multi-disciplinary World Bank implementation support team; Capacity building and technical assistance

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited human resource capacity in federal and state agencies, sustainability of infrastructure investments dependent on adequate management and government commitment
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity-strengthening activities; Technical assistance through third-party service providers; Recruitment of specialized staff in NCO and SCOs

identified_risks.risk_description: Weaknesses in procurement systems, accountability and value-for-money concerns
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Hiring of FM and Procurement Specialists; Capacity building; Enhanced controls; Regular World Bank supervision; Third-party monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential involuntary displacement from land acquisition, infrastructure rehabilitation, and grazing reserve protection; SEA/SH risks from labor influx
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Resettlement Policy Framework; Environmental and Social Management Framework; SEA/SH Action Plan; Grievance Redress Mechanism; Stakeholder Engagement Plan; Labor Management Procedure

identified_risks.risk_description: Diverse stakeholder interests in conflict mitigation, exclusion of deserving states, managing expectations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Consultative approach to conflict prevention; Transparent state selection criteria; Stakeholder engagement throughout implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Escalating conflict and violence across participating states, potential preclosure of implementation areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security Management Plan; Government commitment to security; Project design includes conflict mitigation component; Flexibility in implementation

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Layered GRM at national, state, and local levels with reporting channels that are ethical, confidential, and safe for women and girls to report SEA/SH issues. Procedural manual for grievance redress officers at all levels.
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project targets at least 30% women beneficiaries. Addresses gender gaps in livestock ownership, access to productive resources, and decision-making power. Specific targets: increase women's use of extension services from 8% to 20%, veterinary services from 10% to 30%. Develops financial products using psychometric testing to overcome collateral constraints for women. Plans sex-disaggregated impact evaluation on women's economic empowerment.

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly addresses farmer-herder conflicts driven by climate change and resource scarcity. Component 3 focuses on conflict prevention and mitigation through: establishment of local conflict committees, community dialogue, early warning systems, infrastructure along transhumance corridors, and transnational agreements on livestock mobility. Project incorporates conflict-sensitive approaches and community ownership in peacebuilding.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Nigeria faces significant fragility challenges: (1) Boko Haram insurgency in Northeast; (2) Escalating farmer-herder conflicts across North-Central and southern zones since 2015, killing thousands and costing estimated US$14 billion annually; (3) Banditry and cattle rustling; (4) Climate change-induced resource scarcity driving migration and conflict; (5) Weak governance and institutional capacity; (6) High poverty rates (40%+ of population); (7) Limited access to basic services. Project targets fragile areas and addresses root causes of conflict.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risk rated as High due to escalating conflict and violence. Project includes Security Management Plan to be prepared within 9 months of effectiveness. Implementation flexibility to respond to security changes. Project activities in conflict-prone areas may be precluded by escalation of violence. Government commitment to security is essential. Project design includes conflict mitigation as core component.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - potential displacement. OP 4.12 triggered because project interventions may result in land acquisition, temporary loss of livelihoods, and limited physical resettlement. Specific areas: (1) Livestock Service Centers requiring 100+ hectares of land; (2) Infrastructure rehabilitation and construction; (3) Protection of grazing reserves and livestock routes. Resettlement Policy Framework prepared to guide RAP development. States responsible for resettlement financing. Safeguards screening required for land gazettement.
